content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
March 31,
1992
Alice C. Graff
City Attorney
25151 Clawiter Road
Hayward,
CA 94545
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A-92-134
Dear Ms. Graff:
This letter is in response to your request for confirmation
of the telephone advice provided to you on February 26,
1992,
regarding the filing obligations of public officials who manage
public investments under the provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the “Act”) 1
During the course of our telephone conversation,
you advised
me that the City of Hayward has formed a city staff investment
advisory committee to recommend policies to be followed for the
investment of public funds.
This committee is composed of the
deputy director of finance, the director of public works,
the
personnel director and the assistant city manager.
You also
submitted for our consideration your memorandum dated February 28,
1992,
addressed to these city officials.
Your memorandum
concludes that members of the investment advisory committee are
“public officials who manage public investments,” as that term is
used in Section 87200,
and are thus required to file Commission
Form 721,
Statement of Economic Interests.
The Commission is now in the process of adopting regulations
to define the term “other public officials who manage public
investments,” as that term is used in Section 87200.
We have
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000,
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
A-92-134
Page
2
provided interim advice to the effect that pending adoption of
appropriate regulations, public officials who have been filing
Commission Form 730 Statement of Economic Interests may continue
filing Form 730.
Our interim advice was not intended to preclude a
determination that members of the investment advisory committee
are public officials who manage public investments.
In
conjunction with a determination that a public official manages
public investments,
a public official should change his or her
filing status from Form 730 to Form 721.
Accordingly,
we
reiterate the telephone advice provided to you to the effect that
members of the investment advisory committee of the City of
Hayward who manage public investments should file Commission Form
721.
In the event that a member of the investment advisory
committee has filed Form 730 in compliance with our interim
advice,
such person should amend his or her statement as soon as
possible and disclose his or her economic interests on Form 721.
We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
Should you have any further questions regarding this matter,
do
not hesitate to call me at
(916)
322_5901.2
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
By:
Blanca M.
Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division
SH:BMB:bb
2
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10 per page.

sections.question_synthetic: Are members of the City of Hayward's investment advisory committee considered 'public officials who manage public investments' under Section 87200 of the Political Reform Act, and if so, what filing obligations do they have?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Members of the investment advisory committee who manage public investments are public officials under Section 87200 and must file Commission Form 721 (Statement of Economic Interests) rather than Form 730. Those who previously filed Form 730 should amend their statements and switch to Form 721 as soon as possible.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is confirmation of prior telephone advice. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present, but question and conclusion are clearly identifiable from letter content. Interim regulations mentioned as being pending adoption.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Are members of the City of Hayward's investment advisory committee considered 'public officials who manage public investments' under Section 87200 of the Political Reform Act, and if so, what filing obligations do they have?

CONCLUSION: Members of the investment advisory committee who manage public investments are public officials under Section 87200 and must file Commission Form 721 (Statement of Economic Interests) rather than Form 730. Those who previously filed Form 730 should amend their statements and switch to Form 721 as soon as possible.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission March 31, 1992 Alice C. Graff City Attorney 25151 Clawiter Road Hayward, CA 94545 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. A-92-134 Dear Ms. Graff: This letter is in response to your request for confirmation of the telephone advice provided to you on February 26, 1992, regarding the filing obligations of public officials who manage public investments under the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”) 1 During the course of our telephone conversation, you advised me that the City of Hayward has formed a city staff investment advisory committee to recommend policies to be followed for the investment of public funds. This committee is composed of the deputy director of finance, the director of public works, the personnel director and the assistant city manager. You also submitted for our consideration your memorandum dated February 28, 1992, addressed to these city officials. Your memorandum concludes that members of the investment advisory committee are “public officials who manage public investments,” as that term is used in Section 87200, and are thus required to file Commission Form 721, Statement of Economic Interests. The Commission is now in the process of adopting regulations to define the term “other public officials who manage public investments,” as that term is used in Section 87200. We have 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 Our File No. A-92-134 Page 2 provided interim advice to the effect that pending adoption of appropriate regulations, public officials who have been filing Commission Form 730 Statement of Economic Interests may continue filing Form 730. Our interim advice was not intended to preclude a determination that members of the investment advisory committee are public officials who manage public investments. In conjunction with a determination that a public official manages public investments, a public official should change his or her filing status from Form 730 to Form 721. Accordingly, we reiterate the telephone advice provided to you to the effect that members of the investment advisory committee of the City of Hayward who manage public investments should file Commission Form 721. In the event that a member of the investment advisory committee has filed Form 730 in compliance with our interim advice, such person should amend his or her statement as soon as possible and disclose his or her economic interests on Form 721. We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry. Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do not hesitate to call me at (916) 322_5901.2 Sincerely, Scott Hallabrin Acting General Counsel By: Blanca M. Breeze Counsel, Legal Division SH:BMB:bb 2 Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials
embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice that members of the City of Hayward's investment advisory committee are public officials who manage public investments and must file Form 721 instead of Form 730, with instructions to amend any previously filed Form 730 statements.
